description.title: High-gain coupled carbon markets — the damping demonstrator
description.economic_framing: The same two-coupled-carbon-market structure as joint_two_market.json, but with a HIGH cross-market gain: market B's abatement cost is very sensitive to market A's price (phi_BA=3.0), while A's cost falls as B's price rises (phi_AB=-0.5). The loop gain g = phi_AB*phi_BA = -1.50 has magnitude > 1, so undamped Gauss-Seidel iteration OSCILLATES (period-2) and never settles; only relaxation (under-damping the price update) recovers the joint equilibrium. This mirrors anchor J2 of docs/joint-equilibrium.md.
description.mechanism: Two interior threshold markets pin at their (link-shifted) block costs (s_m=1): P_A = 100 - 0.5*P_B, P_B = 100 + 3.0*P_A. The unique joint fixed point is P_A=20, P_B=160 USD/tCO2. SHIPPED at joint_solver.relaxation=0.5, whose relaxed-Gauss-Seidel eigenvalue is -0.25 (|.|<1) so the CONFIG CONVERGES to (20, 160). At relaxation=1.0 (undamped) the iteration eigenvalue is g=-1.5: the price pair ping-pongs (A alternates ~50<->0 as its shifted block cost is driven negative and clipped at the P->0 oversupply boundary, B alternates ~250<->100), the solver stamps Joint Converged=0 and Joint Cycle Detected=2, and NO faked equilibrium number is reported. Damping is the remedy, not more iterations.

expected_output.reading: A single knob (relaxation) turns a divergent high-gain cycle into a convergent one. The engine never reports the last iterate of an oscillating run as an equilibrium; it stamps Joint Converged=0 and flags the period-2 cycle so the modeller knows to damp rather than iterate.

scenarios.name: Coupled carbon markets (high-gain, damped to converge)
